page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-80 is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the n4d family. It features 80 dedicated virtual central processing units (vCPUs) based on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 320.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU core.

The instance does not include integrated graphics processing units (GPUs) or local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be fulfilled via external network-attached storage options. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and standard 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio provide a predictable resource density for multi-threaded workloads. This profile is designed for general-purpose applications, enterprise web serving, and distributed containerized environments that require high core counts without specialized hardware acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-80 is a general-purpose server instance configured with 80 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 320.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance features no local storage and zero integrated GPUs, relying on external network storage for data persistence. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for multi-threaded workloads, the dedicated CPU allocation offers predictable resource density. This server type is suitable for general-purpose workloads, enterprise web serving, and containerized applications requiring substantial parallel processing capacity without specialized hardware accelerators.
